segments.text: Hey, I'm Manuel, and this is the BDR pipeline I built as a proof artifact for AI-assisted
go-to-market workflows.
The idea is simple.
Take a target company, enrich it with public signals, choose a relevant outreach angle,
generate a multi-touch sequence and run a quality critique before a human reviews anything.
I want to be clear about the claim.
This is not a revenue case study.
I do not have campaign metrics or permission to show private client data, so this public
version uses anonymized demo companies.
The honest claim is, I built a multi-agent video workflow that turns a target company
into a researched outreach using live signals, tenant-specific positioning, sequence generation
and a critic pass.
The problem is that outbound prep is just straight up slow.
For each account, someone has a research company, understand what is happening to the business.
Find the right persona, choose an angle, write the first email, write follow-ups and check
whether it sounds human.
That can easily take up to 20 to 30 minutes per account.
So I build a repeatable workflow instead of a one-off prompt.
The app uses streamlets for the interface and land graph for orchestration.
The first stage is enrichment.
The system pulls public company signals with EXR and uses hot.io for contact information
discovery when obviously real data exists and uses Claude to summarize the accounts through
configured ICP and persona.
Next, the strategist chooses one of the three tenant-defined outreach angles that matters
because good outbound is not just better wording, it's choosing the right reason to reach
out.
Then, the humanizer creates an outreach package.
The model generates specific observations, but proof points, CTAs, subject lines and follow-up
structures come from tenant-copy banks.
That keeps the output more consistent and less generic.
The pipeline then creates a sequence, LinkedIn Connect, first email, follow-up, social proof,
LinkedIn DM and break up email.
Finally, the critic scores each touch on pain specificity, proof relevance, CTA clarity
and human voice.
The output is not autosent, it's a research first draft for human to review and edit.
All the public demo are using fictional companies, so contact discovery is not being found
here, but it will be found when using real companies as X, as hunter.io is very reliable.
The workflow works quite well, but this is just the first draft and the things I would like
to improve are reply tracking, CRM export, campaign analytics, better ICP scoring and an
EVA system which learns from previous runs to improve each run after each other.
But now this workflow just shows at a low level and a service level how AI workflows can
improve real business problems.
